steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: c = cans at \$1.87 each, t = bags of treats at \$2.37 each. The shopper can buy the items only if the total cost stays within the \$20 budget, so total cost ≤ 20. The correct inequality assigns the right price to each variable and uses ≤.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — $1.87c + 2.37t \leq 20$: \$1.87 per can times c cans plus \$2.37 per bag times t bags, total at most \$20. ✓
  • Choice B — $1.87c + 2.37t \geq 20$: correct coefficients but wrong direction — ≥ would mean the shopper must spend at least \$20, not stay under it. ✗
  • Choice C — $2.37c + 1.87t \leq 20$: swaps the prices — assigns \$2.37 to cans and \$1.87 to bags, opposite of the given information. ✗
  • Choice D — $2.37c + 1.87t \geq 20$: wrong coefficients and wrong inequality direction. ✗
That is the correct answer.
